segments.text: My eyes have seen all this, my ears have heard and understood it.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But I desire to speak to the Almighty and to argue my case with God.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You, however, smear me with lies; you are worthless physicians, all of you!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If only you would be altogether silent! For you, that would be wisdom.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Hear now my argument; listen to the pleas of my lips.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will you speak wickedly on God's behalf? Will you speak deceitfully for him?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will you show him partiality? Will you argue the case for God?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Would it turn out well if he examined you? Could you deceive him as you might deceive a mortal?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He would surely call you to account if you secretly showed partiality.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Would not his splendor terrify you? Would not the dread of him fall on you?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes; your defenses are defenses of clay.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Keep silent and let me speak; then let come to me what may.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do I put myself in jeopardy and take my life in my hands?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Though he slay me, yet will I hope in him; I will surely defend my ways to his face.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Indeed, this will turn out for my deliverance, for no godless person would dare come before him!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Listen carefully to what I say; let my words ring in your ears.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Now that I have prepared my case, I know I will be vindicated.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Can anyone bring charges against me? If so, I will be silent and die.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Only grant me these two things, God, and then I will not hide from you:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Withdraw your hand far from me, and stop frightening me with your terrors.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then summon me and I will answer, or let me speak, and you reply to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How many wrongs and sins have I committed? Show me my offense and my sin.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do you hide your face and consider me your enemy?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will you torment a windblown leaf? Will you chase after dry chaff?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For you write down bitter things against me and make me reap the sins of my youth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You fasten my feet in shackles; you keep close watch on all my paths by putting marks on the soles of my feet.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: So man wastes away like something rotten, like a garment eaten by moths.
